TECHNICAL EXPERIENCE

Selected for an intern conference in Charlotte, NC.
Experienced office work and computational work related to geotechnical engineering.
References: Dave Ferko ((757) 620-1400), GEotechnical Department Manager; Greg Lewis ((202) 669-5755), Project Manager.



Honors Thesis Author | “Potential causes of clustered knickpoints in the Appalachian Mountains”

Scientific writing and figure making for the Geology department.
Working with large datasets and manipulating large \((>200k~km^{2})\) rasters.
Experience writing proposals, presenting results, and organizing large coding projects.

title: Adaptive Groundwater Monitoring in Heterogeneous Aquifers
Nick Lewis
text:
Geomorphologist / GIS Analyst
Leveraging field and computational research experience for data-driven analysis. ntompkinslew@gmail.com | GitHub | LinkedIn | (757) 667-8823

title: Nick Lewis
text:
About me




Geology, coding, and data science.
For the last 2 years, I have been working with Joanmarie Del Vecchio and Chuck Bailey on an honors thesis in the geology department at William & Mary: Potential causes of clustered knickpoints in the Appalachians. The study entailed raster analysis, data management, scientific writing, proposal writing, and figure making.













Passion for cartography
I find great enjoyement in creating visually pleasing and artistic mpas that display data in pragmatic, clear ways.









Music
When im not working, I make bad rock music here.
